skill: Nonlinear equations in one variable and systems of equations in two variables
stem:
3x/(x+6) = 1 - 6/(x+6)

What is the solution to the equation above?
rationale: Choice C is correct. The right side can be written as (x+6)/(x+6) - 6/(x+6) = x/(x+6). Since both sides share the denominator (x+6), the numerators must be equal: 3x = x. Subtracting x from both sides yields 2x = 0, so x = 0. Verifying: when x = 0, LHS = 0/6 = 0 and RHS = 1 - 6/6 = 0. Choice A is incorrect since x = -6 makes the denominator zero. Choices B and D do not satisfy the equation.
